---
title: Environment Variables
description: Complete reference for all environment variables in DevPush
---

All environment variables are configured in your `.env` file (typically at `/var/lib/devpush/.env` in production or `./data/.env` in development).

## Auto-Generated Variables

These variables are automatically generated during installation and should not be manually set.

<ParamField path="SECRET_KEY" type="string" required>
  App secret for sessions and CSRF protection. Auto-generated by `install.sh`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="ENCRYPTION_KEY" type="string" required>
  Fernet key for encrypting secrets (OAuth tokens, environment variables, etc.). Auto-generated by `install.sh`.
  
  To generate manually:
  ```bash
  python -c 'from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())'
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="POSTGRES_PASSWORD" type="string" required>
  PostgreSQL database password. Auto-generated by `install.sh`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SERVICE_UID" type="integer" default="1000">
  Container user UID. Auto-set to match host user during installation.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SERVICE_GID" type="integer" default="1000">
  Container user GID. Auto-set to match host user during installation.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SERVER_IP" type="string" default="127.0.0.1">
  Public IP address of the server. Auto-detected by `install.sh`.
</ParamField>

## Required Configuration

These variables must be set for DevPush to function properly.

### Domains & SSL

<ParamField path="APP_HOSTNAME" type="string" required>
  Domain for the application UI (e.g., `devpush.example.com` or `example.com`).
  
  Set an `A` record pointing this domain to your server IP.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DEPLOY_DOMAIN" type="string" required>
  Domain for deployments (wildcard root). Set explicitly with no default.
  
  Example: `deploy.example.com` or `example.com`
  
  Set a wildcard `A` record: `*.deploy.example.com` → server IP
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="LE_EMAIL" type="string" required>
  Email address for Let's Encrypt SSL certificate notifications and renewal alerts.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER" type="string" default="default">
  ACME challenge provider for SSL certificate validation.
  
  **Options:**
  - `default` — HTTP-01 challenge (simplest, works for most cases)
  - `cloudflare` — DNS-01 via Cloudflare (requires `CF_DNS_API_TOKEN`)
  - `route53` — DNS-01 via AWS Route 53
  - `gcloud` — DNS-01 via Google Cloud DNS
  - `digitalocean` — DNS-01 via DigitalOcean
  - `azure` — DNS-01 via Azure DNS
  
  DNS-01 is required for wildcard certificates or when HTTP-01 is not feasible.
</ParamField>

### GitHub App

Required for GitHub OAuth login and repository access. See the [GitHub App setup guide](https://devpu.sh/docs/guides/create-github-app).

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_ID" type="string" required>
  GitHub App ID from your GitHub App settings.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_NAME" type="string" required>
  GitHub App name (slug).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY" type="string" required>
  GitHub App private key in PEM format. Use `\n` for newlines when setting in `.env`.
  
  Example:
  ```bash
  GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIE...\n-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----"
  ```
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET" type="string" required>
  GitHub webhook secret for verifying webhook payloads.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_CLIENT_ID" type="string" required>
  GitHub OAuth client ID for user authentication.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GITHUB_APP_CLIENT_SECRET" type="string" required>
  GitHub OAuth client secret.
</ParamField>

### Email

Required for sending login emails and team invitations.

<ParamField path="EMAIL_SENDER_ADDRESS" type="string" required>
  Email address used as the sender for login emails and invitations.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="RESEND_API_KEY" type="string">
  API key for [Resend](https://resend.com) email service. Optional if SMTP is configured.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SMTP_HOST" type="string">
  SMTP server hostname. When set with username/password, SMTP is used instead of Resend.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SMTP_PORT" type="integer" default="587">
  SMTP server port.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SMTP_USERNAME" type="string">
  SMTP authentication username. Required when using SMTP.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="SMTP_PASSWORD" type="string">
  SMTP authentication password. Required when using SMTP.
</ParamField>

## Optional Configuration

### OAuth Providers

<ParamField path="GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID" type="string">
  Google OAuth client ID for Google Sign-In integration (optional).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET" type="string">
  Google OAuth client secret.
</ParamField>

### Branding

<ParamField path="APP_NAME" type="string" default="/dev/push">
  Display name for the application shown in the UI.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="APP_DESCRIPTION" type="string">
  Application description (used in meta tags and other places).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="EMAIL_SENDER_NAME" type="string" default="/dev/push">
  Display name for the email sender.
</ParamField>

### Database

<ParamField path="POSTGRES_DB" type="string" default="devpush">
  PostgreSQL database name.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="POSTGRES_USER" type="string" default="devpush-app">
  PostgreSQL database user.
</ParamField>

### Infrastructure

<ParamField path="REDIS_URL" type="string" default="redis://redis:6379">
  Redis connection URL for job queue and caching.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DOCKER_HOST" type="string" default="tcp://docker-proxy:2375">
  Docker API endpoint for managing deployment containers.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DATA_DIR" type="string" default="/var/lib/devpush">
  Data directory path for uploads, Traefik config, logs, and other persistent data.
  
  In development, defaults to `./data`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="APP_DIR" type="string" default="/opt/devpush">
  Application code directory.
  
  In development, defaults to the project root.
</ParamField>

### Resource Limits

<ParamField path="DEFAULT_CPUS" type="float">
  Default CPU limit per deployment (e.g., `0.5` for half a CPU core, `2.0` for two cores).
  
  If not set, deployments have no CPU limit.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="MAX_CPUS" type="float">
  Maximum allowed CPU override per project. Only used when `DEFAULT_CPUS` is set.
  
  Required to let users customize CPU limits in project settings.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DEFAULT_MEMORY_MB" type="integer">
  Default memory limit in megabytes per deployment (e.g., `2048` for 2GB).
  
  If not set, deployments have no memory limit.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="MAX_MEMORY_MB" type="integer">
  Maximum allowed memory override per project in megabytes. Only used when `DEFAULT_MEMORY_MB` is set.
  
  Required to let users customize memory limits in project settings.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DEFAULT_DB_SIZE_LIMIT_BYTES" type="integer" default="5368709120">
  Default database size limit in bytes per project.
  
  Default is 5GB (5 × 1024 × 1024 × 1024).
</ParamField>

### Timeouts & Scheduling

<ParamField path="JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" type="integer" default="320">
  Maximum execution time for background jobs in seconds.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="JOB_MAX_TRIES" type="integer" default="3">
  Maximum number of retry attempts for failed background jobs.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS" type="integer" default="300">
  Maximum time allowed for a deployment to complete in seconds (5 minutes).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="CONTAINER_DELETE_GRACE_SECONDS" type="integer" default="15">
  Wait time in seconds before deleting containers after stop/failure. Allows logs to be shipped to Loki.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="LOG_STREAM_GRACE_SECONDS" type="integer" default="5">
  Grace window in seconds for deployment log streaming. Determines when to connect/close SSE streams around terminal states.
</ParamField>

### Authentication & Security

<ParamField path="MAGIC_LINK_TTL_SECONDS" type="integer" default="900">
  Magic link validity duration in seconds (15 minutes).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="AUTH_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS" type="integer" default="30">
  Auth cookie/JWT lifetime in days.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="AUTH_TOKEN_REFRESH_THRESHOLD_DAYS" type="integer" default="1">
  Refresh auth token when expiring within N days.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="AUTH_TOKEN_ISSUER" type="string" default="devpush-app">
  JWT issuer claim for auth tokens.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="AUTH_TOKEN_AUDIENCE" type="string" default="devpush-web">
  JWT audience claim for auth tokens.
</ParamField>

### Logging & Debugging

<ParamField path="LOG_LEVEL" type="string" default="WARNING">
  Application logging level.
  
  **Options:** `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARNING`, `ERROR`, `CRITICAL`
</ParamField>

## DNS Provider-Specific Variables

Required when using DNS-01 challenge for SSL certificates.

### Cloudflare

<ParamField path="CF_DNS_API_TOKEN" type="string">
  Cloudflare API token with `Zone:DNS:Edit` permissions.
  
  Required when `CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER=cloudflare`.
</ParamField>

### AWS Route 53

Configure AWS credentials via standard environment variables or IAM role when using `CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER=route53`.

### Google Cloud DNS

Configure GCP credentials via service account when using `CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER=gcloud`.

### DigitalOcean

Configure DigitalOcean API token when using `CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER=digitalocean`.

### Azure DNS

Configure Azure credentials when using `CERT_CHALLENGE_PROVIDER=azure`.

## Configuration File Location

**Production:**
```
/var/lib/devpush/.env
```

**Development:**
```
./data/.env
```

## Example Configuration

See `.env.example` in the repository for a complete example with all available variables.
